decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent. Thoughtseize is used for disruption and Bitter Triumph provides additional removal. The deck includes various mana-fixing lands to ensure smooth mana curves. Formidable Speaker and Raffine's Informant are key cards for generating value and card advantage. Witherbloom Command and Cache Grab provide additional utility options. The goal is to control the board early and then overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and synergistic plays.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays using a mix of creatures, removal spells, and disruption to control the game and attack the opponent's life total. It aims to pressure the opponent quickly with creatures like Bloodtithe Harvester, Bonecrusher Giant, and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress. The deck's mana base is streamlined to support the aggressive strategy, with a mix of lands that can produce the necessary colors of mana and utility lands like Mutavault for additional threats.

This UR aggro deck focuses on using low-cost spells and creatures to quickly apply pressure to the opponent. The deck utilizes cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger to deal damage efficiently, while also running spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to remove opposing threats. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent early in the game and maintain board control through a combination of efficient creatures and burn spells, ultimately winning through aggressive combat damage.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to consistently play low-cost spells and creatures to deal rapid damage to the opponent. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Fiery Impulse, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game. With a focus on tempo and aggression, this deck looks to establish board presence quickly and maintain pressure throughout the game. Additionally, cards like Stock Up and Riverpyre Verge provide card advantage and flexibility to adapt to different situations.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures and disruptive spells to pressure opponents early in the game. By curving out with powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition while taxing the opponent with Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and close out the game quickly. Collected Company provides additional creature advantage, while cards like Aven Interrupter and Michelangelo's Technique offer additional utility to push through damage. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a steady stream of threats and disruption.

This deck focuses on utilizing control elements and ramping up mana to power out powerful threats such as Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. It includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and removal options like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push to control the game state. The deck also includes ramp creatures like Llanowar Elves and powerful lands like Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to accelerate the mana development. By combining efficient removal, disruption, and ramp, this deck aims to take over the game and outvalue the opponent with strong threats.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control elements and efficient removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also deploying creatures with graveyard synergy to generate advantage and close out the game. It aims to grind out value over time by constantly disrupting the opponent's resources and leveraging its own graveyard interactions. With a mix of hand disruption, removal, and resilient threats, it can adapt to various matchups and strategies, ultimately outlasting the opponent through attrition and strategic play.

The deck focuses on maintaining control of the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card draw. It also utilizes the companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard for additional value. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by efficiently dealing with threats and gaining card advantage, while also having the flexibility to adapt to different matchups with its versatile card choices and strong late-game threats like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. The mana base is built to support the deck's color requirements and provide consistent access to its spells.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy with a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. It aims to control the game by using cards like Unholy Annex and Culling Ritual to outvalue the opponent. The creatures in the deck, such as Badgermole Cub and Graveyard Trespasser, provide additional threats while also fueling graveyard synergies. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to close out the game.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using a mix of counterspells, card draw, and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes a few powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to generate value and provide additional control options. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds an extra layer of versatility to the deck. The aim is to outlast the opponent, establish board control, and eventually win through incremental advantage and powerful finishers.

The deck revolves around assembling the Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II combo to generate powerful synergies and win conditions. It includes various support cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push for disruption and removal, as well as Iron-Shield Elf for protection. The mana base is designed to support the combo and ensure consistency in executing the game plan. Overall, the deck focuses on setting up the combo pieces efficiently while controlling the board and disrupting opponents to secure victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game using a variety of spells, creatures, and lands to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating card advantage with Niv-Mizzet Reborn. The deck features a diverse mana base to support the multiple colors needed for its powerful cards. With a mix of removal, ramp, and powerful threats, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and secure victory through superior card advantage and board control. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion provides additional value and flexibility in longer games.

The main concept of this deck is to use low-cost aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter to quickly take control of the board and deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes a variety of instant spells like Fiery Impulse and Stock Up to remove threats and protect your creatures. The use of cards like Vivi Ornitier and Riverpyre Verge helps to maintain card advantage and ensure consistency in draws. Overall, the deck aims to apply continuous pressure on the opponent while maintaining control of the battlefield.

This Rakdos Aggro deck focuses on aggressive plays and disrupting the opponent's game plan. It utilizes cards like Bloodtithe Harvester and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker to apply pressure, while also including disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress to control the opponent's hand. The deck also has removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrade to deal with opposing threats. With a mix of creatures, disruption, and removal, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and secure victories through aggressive plays and disruption.

This deck focuses on using a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and efficient creatures to control the game and out-value the opponent. By utilizing cards like Abrupt Decay, Fatal Push, and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan, as well as powerful threats like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to close out the game, this deck aims to gradually grind down the opponent's resources and establish board control. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber and Tear Asunder provide additional utility and flexibility in dealing with various threats.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly cycle through cards using spells like Treasure Cruise and Consider to fill your graveyard with spells and trigger Arclight Phoenix's ability to return from the graveyard to the battlefield. Utilizing efficient removal spells like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse, along with disruptive cards like Ledger Shredder and Picklock Prankster, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive plays while also maintaining card advantage and disrupting their game plan. It's all about efficient spellcasting and graveyard manipulation to bring Phoenix back and secure victory.
